title: The Dark Loam Archive

features.name: soil-memory
features.desc-on-touch: You press into earth that holds the names of everything it has touched; the soil has learned to address what it feeds.

features.name: root-bundles
features.desc-on-touch: Bound threads of ancient root, still conducting signals down into the dark; they remember where every drink came from.

features.name: earth-letters
features.desc-on-touch: A filing system where soil layers hold the return addresses of everything it has buried; the archive reads itself one grain at a time.

features.name: mineral-hum
features.desc-on-touch: You hear it through your feet: iron speaking to salt, salt answering quartz; the earth's slow commerce, the soil's only broadcast.

features.name: ferment-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Dark water held in clay; the soil teaching itself to become something else; organisms making comparison into flavour, corruption into growth.

features.name: buried-vessels
features.desc-on-touch: Stacked clay containers, some full of seed, some full of absence; the archive stored things here when it still believed burial meant preservation.

inscription: soil that learned to archive itself; now every grain holds a neighbor's address

file.name: Soil_Composition_NSW_Depth_Meters_1-12.mp3
file.note: field recordings from bore holes; soil singing its own stratigraphy; layers confusing timestamps; deeper strata remember futures

exits.label: back up through root-garden toward salt and grain
deeper into earth where writing becomes a language of stones
exits.to: NEW: where earth's inscription reaches bedrock and becomes unchangeable

exits.label: toward the archive's own earth-memory, its foundation
into root-tangle and the silence of growing things
exits.to: NEW: the root-garden where nothing grows but everything listens
